abstract: In scientific literature, graphical representations are indispensable for distilling and communicating complex datasets. However, the utility of existing visualization tools is frequently constrained by steep learning curves, high costs, and platform-specific limitations. To address these challenges, we developed SRplot, a comprehensive and accessible web-based platform that integrates over a hundred essential data visualization and graphing functions. SRplot is compatible with all modern web browsers and requires minimal local computing power, making high-quality visualization accessible to all researchers. Featuring an intuitive graphical interface, the platform allows users to simply paste their data into formatted text boxes to generate real-time, customizable figures. The resulting outputs are available in both publication-quality bitmap (PNG, TIFF) and vector (PDF, SVG) formats. We maintain a commitment to continuous improvement, regularly updating and optimizing SRplot’s functionality based on direct community feedback. To date, graphs generated via SRplot have been featured in over five hundred peer-reviewed publications. The SRplot web server is freely available for use at http://www.bioinformatics.com.cn/SRplot.
